name: oil_shock_equity_drawdown
description: Oil headlines push crude sharply higher intraday, then index breadth deteriorates and yields rise; broad equities sell off into the close.
notes: Relevant now because Brent settled at 112.19 and WTI at 98.32 on March 20 while S&P 500 closed 6,506.48 and 10-year yields jumped to 4.38%.

name: defensive_rotation_over_growth
description: Capital rotates into energy, industrials, and consumer defensives while technology and other long-duration growth cohorts lag.
notes: Relevant now because 2026 leadership has shifted to real-economy sectors, with energy and defensives outperforming and Nasdaq down more than broad indices YTD.

name: high_vix_failed_breakout
description: Initial upside breakout clears intraday highs but quickly fails as volatility stays elevated and buyers do not get follow-through.
notes: Relevant now because VIX moved from mid-teens in January to a 29.49 spike in early March and remains in the mid-20s, conditions that historically increase whipsaw risk.

name: spot_down_vol_down_hedge_unwind
description: Index price drifts lower but implied volatility softens as participants monetize existing hedges rather than add fresh downside protection.
notes: Relevant now because Cboe's Macro Volatility Digest explicitly flags a recent 'spot down, vol down' dynamic during oil-driven headline swings.

name: fed_hold_cut_repricing
description: Policy hold with hawkish uncertainty removes near-term cut expectations, lifting yields and pressuring valuation-sensitive equities.
notes: Relevant now because the Fed held at 3.50-3.75% on March 18 and market commentary indicates traders have removed most 2026 cut bets.
